description: Concise, source-checked summaries and relevance notes for public corpus cards. A note qualifies this project's classification or use of a source; it does not assert that a published proof is false.

2607.01126.resultSummary: Assuming the Extended Riemann Hypothesis, studies Selmer-rank distributions in cyclic p-extensions and derives probabilistic rank bounds for elliptic, superelliptic, and hyperelliptic curves.
2607.01126.note: This is a conditional study of Selmer ranks, not a contour-shifting or error-term result for H14. Its former H14 classification was unsupported and has been changed to Context only.

2607.09797.resultSummary: Assuming simple zeta zeros, gives an explicit formula for the Möbius discrete Laplace transform; an O(x⁻¹ᐟ²) bound implies RH, while the converse needs additional hypotheses on the zeros.
2607.09797.note: The explicit formula assumes simple zeros. The stated RH criterion is one-way unconditionally; this paper is not a standalone unconditional proof of RH.
